transcript.content: Webhook Dedup is creating duplicate customer-visible effects. Please stop it quickly; symptom: Duplicate webhook creates a second order.

# webhook dedup
# Symptom: Duplicate webhook creates a second order.
# Regression: Duplicate webhook creates a second order.
The repo has only stub files, so I can't fix at source right now — the priority is to stop customer-visible duplicate orders. I'll disable the broken webhook dedup path via flag and file a follow-up.

args.reason: Webhook dedup logic is producing duplicate orders (customer-visible). Disabled webhook_dedup flag as immediate mitigation. Source code in webhooks.py is a stub — need real implementation with idempotency key (e.g., webhook delivery id) stored transactionally before order creation, so retries/duplicates are no-ops. Follow-up to implement and re-enable flag.

args.summary: Mitigated customer-visible duplicate-order incident by disabling the `webhook_dedup` feature flag (kill-switch). Repo contains only stubs (webhooks.py, tests/test_dedupe.py), so no source fix possible right now; filed an incident exception describing the proper fix — implement idempotency keyed on the webhook delivery id, persisted transactionally before order creation, so duplicate deliveries become no-ops — to be done before re-enabling the flag.
